Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if patient symptomatic of anaemia or Hb < 8g/dL

Vincristine: bilirubin >51 and normal AST/ALT: reduce to 50%
Vincristine: bilirubin >51 and AST/ALT >180: omit
Cyclophosphamide hepatic impairment: clinical decision. Evidence that exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary.
Cyclophosphamide: CrCl 10-20 ml/min: 75% dose
Cyclophosphamide: CrCl <10 ml/min: omit

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence. Always take consultant advice before reducing.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence.
Reduce dose to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine dose from 2mg to 1mg. For higher toxicity grades or if toxicity increases despite dose reduction, stop vincristine.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below then reduce to 75% of the original dose or discontinue as appropriate. Not applicable to alopecia, altered taste or nail changes.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophil >= 1.5x10⁹/L and platelets >= 100x10⁹/L. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL. If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment.

If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval
Growth factors per local formulary: filgrastim 30MU SC once daily x7 days from day 7, OR lenograstim 33.6MU SC once daily x7 days from day 7, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC once on day 6

indication: Relapsed metastatic osteosarcoma; Selected metastatic soft tissue sarcomas (third line) / uterine leiomyosarcoma; Relapsed Ewings (if other second line therapy is not suitable)

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded according to agreed bands. Starting dose 675mg/m²; may increase to 900mg/m² in patients who tolerate or who have had no previous treatment and/or PS 0. In this regimen gemcitabine is administered over 90 minutes.

Dose banded according to agreed bands. Starting dose 75mg/m²; may increase to 100mg/m² in tolerant patients or PS 0 with no prior treatment. Exfoliant. Dexamethasone pre-medication mandatory to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 and day 8
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous

pre_medications.timing: Rescue: stat 15-30 minutes before docetaxel if patient forgot oral pre-medication
pre_medications.special_instructions: Rescue dose only if oral dexamethasone pre-medication forgotten. Standard regimen: dexamethasone 8mg oral twice daily for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (days 7, 8, 9 of cycle) - dispensed as take-home on day 1.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 3 days starting the day before docetaxel (days 7, 8, 9 of the cycle)
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: To prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity reactions. Dispensed on day 1.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day when necessary for the relief of nausea
take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Supply 28 tablets or nearest equivalent pack size. Dispensed day 1.

dose_modifications.notes: Day 1: delay for one week. If neutrophils recover continue at full dose. If no recovery after two weeks, review treatment.
Day 1: delay for one week. If platelets recover continue at full dose. If no recovery after two weeks, review treatment.
Day 8: administer 75% of the original dose of gemcitabine and docetaxel
Day 8: omit both docetaxel and gemcitabine

dose_modifications.condition: 50-99 x10⁹/L with no evidence of bleeding on day 8
dose_modifications.notes: Day 8: administer 75% of the original dose of gemcitabine and docetaxel
Day 8: omit

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or platelets < 25x10⁹/L for more than 5 days
dose_modifications.notes: Use 25% dose reduction for further cycles. If reoccurrence at lower dose then treatment should be discontinued.

dose_modifications.condition: >= 22 µmol/L and/or AST/ALT >= 3.5xULN and Alk Phos >= 6.0xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Docetaxel: not recommended
Docetaxel: 75% dose
Gemcitabine: initiate treatment with a dose of 800mg/m²
Gemcitabine: CrCl < 30 ml/min: consider dose reduction
Reduce docetaxel from 75mg/m² to 60mg/m² once neuropathy resolved to grade 2 or below. If grade 3 occurred at < 75mg/m² or grade 4 develops, consider stopping.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 cutaneous toxicity on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolves to grade 1 or below then reduce from 75mg/m² to 60mg/m². If occurs at 60mg/m² or no recovery after 2 weeks, stop docetaxel. Grade 4: stop docetaxel.
Delay until grade 1 or below then reduce to 60mg/m². Grade 4 stomatitis: stop treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Excessive lacrimation (related to cumulative docetaxel doses, after median 400mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Try hypromellose 0.3% eye drops four times a day. If ocular irritation continues reduce docetaxel to 60mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below then reduce to 75% of the original dose or discontinue as appropriate.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC prior to day 1 and day 8. LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL.

monitoring_requirements: FBC prior to day 1 and day 8 of treatment
LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of treatment
special_warnings: Dexamethasone 8mg oral twice daily must be prescribed for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (days 7, 8, 9 of cycle) to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity reactions
If patient forgot oral dexamethasone: administer dexamethasone 20mg IV stat 15-30 minutes before docetaxel
Docetaxel hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion - for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema) discontinue infusion immediately
Gemcitabine is administered over 90 minutes in this regimen
Growth factors per local formulary: filgrastim 300mcg SC daily x7 from day 9, OR lenograstim 263mcg SC daily x7 from day 9, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC on day 9
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded according to agreed bands. Starting dose 675mg/m²; may increase to 900mg/m² in patients who tolerate or who have had no previous treatment and/or PS 0. In this regimen gemcitabine is administered over 90 minutes.

indication: First line treatment of advanced soft tissue sarcoma: histologically proven soft tissue sarcoma, locally advanced or metastatic disease, disease not amenable to potentially curative treatment with surgery or radiotherapy, no previous treatment with systemic chemotherapy for soft tissue sarcoma. WHO performance status 0 or 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Dose banded according to national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).

Maximum infusion rate 25mg/min. Doses of 1500mg or higher to be given at 25mg/min. Dose banded. Neutral - extravasation. Followed by maintenance olaratumab (21-day cycles days 1 and 8) until disease progression or intolerance.

pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes prior to olaratumab on days 1 and 8
pre_medications.special_instructions: Olaratumab pre-medication to reduce infusion-related reactions

pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes prior to olaratumab/day 1 antiemetic
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Olaratumab pre-medication and antiemetic.

pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous antiemetic

Three times a day when required for the relief of nausea

Delay treatment until platelets equal to or more than 100x10⁹/L

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenic fever/infection or grade 4 neutropenia > 7 days recurring despite olaratumab dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce olaratumab further to 10mg/kg
Doxorubicin: 75% dose
Doxorubicin: 50% dose
Doxorubicin: 25% dose
Doxorubicin: omit
Doxorubicin: consider dose reduction in severe renal failure

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Consider blood transfusion if Hb < 8g/dL (80g/L). Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or risk factors. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops or LVEF decreases by 20% or more from baseline.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of treatment
Baseline LVEF (MUGA scan) in patients with cardiac history, cardiac risk factors or elderly
Repeat MUGA scan if cardiac toxicity suspected during treatment
Discontinue doxorubicin if ejection fraction < 50% or SF < 28% does not normalise
special_warnings: Doxorubicin is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose: 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy)

Olaratumab infusion-related reactions: for NCI-CTC grade 1 or 2 stop infusion, administer paracetamol 1000mg oral, chlorphenamine 10mg IV, hydrocortisone 100mg IV; restart at 50% infusion rate. Subsequent infusions: use lower rate; infusion duration must not exceed 2 hours.
After cycle 8 (doxorubicin + olaratumab), continue maintenance olaratumab 15mg/kg days 1 and 8 every 21 days until disease progression or intolerance
Olaratumab: limited hepatic/severe renal impairment data - use with caution
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped

required_patient_fields.lvef_percent: LVEF assessment (ECHO or MUGA) required before starting anthracycline. Contraindicated if LVEF <50%.
required_patient_fields.prior_anthracycline_dose_mg_m2: Cumulative prior anthracycline dose (mg/m²) required. Doxorubicin lifetime limit 450-550mg/m² (lower with cardiac risk factors or prior mediastinal radiation). Epirubicin limit 900mg/m².

drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Dose banded according to national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
drugs.notes: Vesicant. Dose banded according to national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).

drugs.special_instructions: Maximum infusion rate 25mg/min. Doses of 1500mg or higher to be given at 25mg/min. Dose banded. Neutral - extravasation. Followed by maintenance olaratumab (21-day cycles days 1 and 8) until disease progression or intolerance.
drugs.notes: Maximum infusion rate 25mg/min. Doses of 1500mg or higher to be given at 25mg/min. Dose banded. Neutral - extravasation. Followed by maintenance olaratumab (21-day cycles days 1 and 8) until disease progression or intolerance.

indication: Adjuvant treatment (maximum 3 years) for high-risk resected GIST based on risk criteria or mutation analysis. First line treatment of KIT (CD117) positive unresectable and/or metastatic GIST at 400mg once daily. WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take once daily with plenty of water and with or after food. Available as 400mg or 100mg tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed.

dose_modifications.notes: 1st occurrence: stop treatment until neutrophils >= 1.5x10⁹/L then resume at 400mg. 2nd occurrence: stop until neutrophils >= 1.5x10⁹/L then resume at reduced 300mg dose.
1st occurrence: stop treatment until platelets >= 75x10⁹/L then resume at 400mg. 2nd occurrence: stop until platelets >= 75x10⁹/L then resume at reduced 300mg dose.
Withhold treatment until bilirubin less than 1.5xULN or ALT/AST less than 2.5xULN, then resume treatment at a reduced dose of 300mg.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Haematological toxicity usually presents within 8 weeks of starting. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin per NICE TA323 if Hb < 8g/dL. Baseline LVEF in patients with known heart disease or elderly.

monitoring_requirements: FBC weekly for 4 weeks after initiation or dose increase; fortnightly for 8 weeks; every 4 weeks for next 12 weeks; then every 12 weeks if stable
LFTs weekly for 4 weeks after initiation or dose increase; fortnightly for 8 weeks; every 4 weeks for next 12 weeks; then every 12 weeks if stable
U&Es and electrolytes every 4 weeks
Baseline LVEF evaluation in patients with known underlying heart disease or elderly
special_warnings: Must only be prescribed under supervision of an oncologist/haematologist
NPSA/2008/RRR001 (oral chemotherapy safety alert) must be followed
Imatinib interacts with many other agents - always check for drug interactions before prescribing any additional medication
Continuation of imatinib recommended only if response achieved within 12 weeks; reassess every 12 weeks thereafter
Not recommended at doses above 400mg once daily for unresectable/metastatic GIST that has progressed on 400mg once daily

drugs.special_instructions: Take once daily with plenty of water and with or after food. Available as 400mg or 100mg tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed.
drugs.notes: Once daily | Take once daily with plenty of water and with or after food. Available as 400mg or 100mg tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Exfoliant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Requires pre and post hydration with furosemide, NaCl 0.9%, KCl and MgSO4. Audiometry required before 3rd and 4th cycles.
Vesicant. 24-hour infusion via central line. Dose banded per agreed bands. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).

High-dose methotrexate. Inflammitant. Urinary pH must be > 7 before starting and maintained throughout. Leucovorin rescue mandatory. Methotrexate levels every 24h from 24h post-infusion until < 0.1 micromol/L. CrCl >= 50ml/min required. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: Day 1 (then 80mg on days 2 and 3) with cisplatin-doxorubicin
pre_medications.special_instructions: 125mg on day 1 then 80mg on days 2 and 3. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to cisplatin-doxorubicin; also prescribed for methotrexate days
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Once daily days 1-3 for cisplatin-doxorubicin. Twice daily for 3 days for methotrexate days.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Twice daily for 3 days each chemotherapy block.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Three times daily for 3 days then as required each block.

pre_medications.timing: Beginning 24 hours after start of methotrexate infusion on days 22 and 29, every 3 hours
pre_medications.special_instructions: Continue every 3 hours IV until methotrexate level < 0.1 micromol/L. May be given orally from dose 5 onwards if tolerated. If methotrexate level > 2 micromol/L additional folinic acid may be necessary - seek senior advice.

pre_medications.timing: Pre-cisplatin hydration on days 1 and 2; also as required during methotrexate for fluid overload
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Also prescribed on inpatient chart for methotrexate hydration as required.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 3-4 days. Full dose unless prior reduction. Repeated delay > 7 days: use growth factors rather than dose reduce. If further delay despite growth factors: reduce cisplatin by 25%.
Delay 3-4 days until criteria met (Plts >= 75x10⁹/L)

Methotrexate: delay only (no dose reductions) until neutrophil >= 0.25x10⁹/L. For NCI-CTC grade 4 febrile neutropenia: prescribe growth factors with next cycle; if 2nd episode despite growth factors reduce cisplatin by 25%.
Methotrexate: delay only (no dose reductions) until platelets >= 50x10⁹/L
Doxorubicin: 75% dose
Doxorubicin: 50% dose
Doxorubicin: 25% dose
Doxorubicin: omit
Methotrexate: 100% dose. Transient increases in bilirubin/transaminases up to 2 weeks post-methotrexate should not trigger stopping treatment.
Methotrexate: 75% dose. Persistent hyperbilirubinaemia > 1.25xULN or grade 3/4 hypertransaminasaemia > 3 weeks: discontinue methotrexate.
Methotrexate: omit
Cisplatin: 75% dose
Cisplatin: 50% dose
Cisplatin: omit
Methotrexate requires CrCl >= 50ml/min - do not administer if below this threshold
Reduce cisplatin by 25% for grade 1 neuropathy. Grade 2 or above: omit cisplatin from all future cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Hearing loss extending to 2kHz or lower (cisplatin)
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue cisplatin if hearing loss extends to 2kHz or lower frequencies
Repeat ECHO or MUGA in 7 days. If normalises, continue. If LVEF does not normalise, omit all further doxorubicin.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: INPATIENT regimen. Drug dosages should be modified as little as possible - prefer delay over dose reduction. Re-evaluate every 3-4 days after a delay. CrCl >= 50ml/min required for methotrexate. Urinary pH must be > 7 before starting methotrexate infusion.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including uric acid and phosphate) prior to day 1
Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or risk factors
Audiometry before 3rd and 4th cycles of doxorubicin and cisplatin
GFR measurement (EDTA or 24-hour urine collection) prior to each methotrexate infusion
Methotrexate levels every 24 hours from 24h post-infusion until < 0.1 micromol/L
Urinary pH every 2 hours minimum until methotrexate level < 0.1 micromol/L
Strict fluid balance chart throughout methotrexate (action required if positive by more than 2kg/L)
special_warnings: INPATIENT REGIMEN - all supportive medicines must be prescribed on inpatient chart
Methotrexate infusion must NOT start until urinary pH is above 7; urinary pH must be maintained throughout infusion
Presence of third fluid compartment (ascites, pleural effusion, oedema) may delay methotrexate clearance and increase toxicity - resolve before administering methotrexate
Folinic acid (leucovorin) rescue: 30mg IV every 3 hours starting 24h after methotrexate infusion until level < 0.1 micromol/L
Central line required for doxorubicin administration
Surgery performed after cycle 2; patients re-weighed after surgery and BSA recalculated. Do not correct BSA for amputation.
Regimen changes at cycles 5 and 6 - ensure correct version prescribes the correct number of cycles. There are multiple versions of this protocol in use.
A significant number of drugs interact with high-dose intravenous methotrexate - always check interactions before prescribing additional medication

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required >= 50ml/min for methotrexate administration (GFR by EDTA or 24-hour urine prior to each methotrexate)
required_patient_fields.urinary_ph: Must be > 7 before starting methotrexate infusion
required_patient_fields.lvef_percent: LVEF assessment (ECHO or MUGA) required before starting anthracycline. Contraindicated if LVEF <50%.
required_patient_fields.prior_anthracycline_dose_mg_m2: Cumulative prior anthracycline dose (mg/m²) required. Doxorubicin lifetime limit 450-550mg/m² (lower with cardiac risk factors or prior mediastinal radiation). Epirubicin limit 900mg/m².

drugs.special_instructions: Exfoliant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Requires pre and post hydration with furosemide, NaCl 0.9%, KCl and MgSO4. Audiometry required before 3rd and 4th cycles.
drugs.notes: Exfoliant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Requires pre and post hydration with furosemide, NaCl 0.9%, KCl and MgSO4. Audiometry required before 3rd and 4th cycles.

drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. 24-hour infusion via central line. Dose banded per agreed bands. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
drugs.notes: Vesicant. 24-hour infusion via central line. Dose banded per agreed bands. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).

drugs.special_instructions: High-dose methotrexate. Inflammitant. Urinary pH must be > 7 before starting and maintained throughout. Leucovorin rescue mandatory. Methotrexate levels every 24h from 24h post-infusion until < 0.1 micromol/L. CrCl >= 50ml/min required. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.
drugs.notes: High-dose methotrexate. Inflammitant. Urinary pH must be > 7 before starting and maintained throughout. Leucovorin rescue mandatory. Methotrexate levels every 24h from 24h post-infusion until < 0.1 micromol/L. CrCl >= 50ml/min required. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. [VERIFIED: Methotrexate 12g/m² is standard high-dose protocol for osteosarcoma — refer to local HD-MTX protocol with mandatory leucovorin rescue]

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Irritant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Consider dose reduction if significant hypoalbuminaemia.
Neutral. Mesna uroprotection required. Co-infused with mesna 3000mg/m² in same bag. Dose banded according to agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: Before ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection bolus. Dose banded per agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: Co-infused with ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: In same bag as ifosfamide. Dose banded per agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: After ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: On day 3 may be substituted by oral mesna 1200mg/m² (rounded upwards to nearest 400mg capsule) given at 0, 2 and 6 hours after end of ifosfamide infusion.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, twice daily for 5 days
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, three times daily for 5 days then as required
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, twice daily for 5 days
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 7 days starting on day 8 of the cycle

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for a week and repeat FBC. If within normal parameters continue with full dose treatment.
Delay for a week and repeat FBC. If within normal parameters continue with a 25% dose reduction.
Delay treatment for a week and repeat FBC. If within normal parameters continue with full dose treatment.
Delay for a week and repeat FBC. If within normal parameters continue with a 25% dose reduction.
Etoposide: 50% dose
Etoposide: clinical decision
Ifosfamide: not recommended
Etoposide: CrCl 15-50: 75% dose
Etoposide: CrCl < 15: 50% dose
Ifosfamide: CrCl 40-59: 70% or consider using ifosfamide at dose of 1500mg/m²
Ifosfamide: CrCl < 40: clinical decision, consider using ifosfamide at a dose of 1500mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 mucositis/GI toxicity - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 neutropenic sepsis - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence
Reduce dose to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence
Consider reducing dose for next cycle. NCI-CTC grade 2 or worsening despite dose reduction: consider stopping ifosfamide.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: INPATIENT regimen. Urine output must be maintained above 100ml/hour. EDTA or calculated CrCl prior to each cycle. Urine dip for protein every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including uric acid, albumin, calcium, magnesium, bicarbonate and phosphate) prior to day 1
Urine dip test for protein every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide administration
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide (urine output > 100ml/hour)
special_warnings: INPATIENT REGIMEN - all supportive medicines must be prescribed on inpatient chart
Mesna uroprotection required: pre-bolus 600mg/m² + co-infusion 3000mg/m² with ifosfamide + post-infusion 1800mg/m² over 12 hours
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk factors: low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor performance status, CNS tumour, bulky pelvic disease, concomitant psychotropic drugs, younger age
Methylene blue 50mg four times a day IV in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 30 minutes may prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy
Growth factors per local formulary: filgrastim 30MU SC daily from day 6, OR lenograstim 33.6MU SC daily from day 6, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC once on day 4
Palliative intent

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: EDTA or calculated CrCl required prior to each cycle

drugs.special_instructions: Irritant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Consider dose reduction if significant hypoalbuminaemia.
drugs.notes: Irritant. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Consider dose reduction if significant hypoalbuminaemia.

drugs.special_instructions: Neutral. Mesna uroprotection required. Co-infused with mesna 3000mg/m² in same bag. Dose banded according to agreed bands.
drugs.notes: Neutral. Mesna uroprotection required. Co-infused with mesna 3000mg/m² in same bag. Dose banded according to agreed bands.

indication: First or second line treatment of sarcoma in patients with a cardiac impairment requiring an anthracycline. WHO performance status 0,1,2. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Exfoliant. INCOMPATIBLE with sodium chloride 0.9% - use glucose 5% ONLY; always use glucose 5% flush. First infusion: maximum rate 1mg/minute. Subsequent infusions may be given over 60 minutes (default Aria time 120 minutes). Do NOT use in-line filters. Doses >= 90mg: dilute in 500ml glucose 5%. Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Dose banded according to agreed bands. Not a licensed indication for Caelyx.
Neutral. Mesna uroprotection required. Co-infused with mesna 3000mg/m² in same bag. Dose banded according to agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: Before ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection bolus. Dose banded per agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: Co-infused with ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: In same bag as ifosfamide. Dose banded per agreed bands.

pre_medications.timing: After ifosfamide on days 1, 2, 3
pre_medications.special_instructions: On day 3 may be substituted by oral mesna 1200mg/m² (rounded upwards to nearest 400mg capsule) at 0, 2 and 6 hours after end of ifosfamide infusion.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, twice daily for 5 days
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, three times daily for 5 days then as required
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, twice daily for 5 days
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous. Prescribed on inpatient chart.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice a day for 7 days starting on day 8 of the cycle

dose_modifications.notes: Prior to cycle 1: neutrophil equal to or more than 1x10⁹/L required
Prior to cycle 1: platelets equal to or more than 100x10⁹/L required
Delay until recovery to 1x10⁹/L or greater then continue at full dose
Delay until recovery to 1x10⁹/L or greater then continue at 75% of the original dose
Delay until recovery to 100x10⁹/L or greater then continue at full dose
Delay until recovery to 100x10⁹/L or greater then continue at 75% of the original dose
Ifosfamide: not recommended
Liposomal doxorubicin: bilirubin less than 20: 100% dose
Liposomal doxorubicin: 75% dose (initial dosing). May be increased to 100% at next cycle if well tolerated with minimal toxicity and no increase in bilirubin/liver enzymes.
Liposomal doxorubicin: 50% dose (initial dosing). May be increased to 75% at next cycle if well tolerated.
Ifosfamide: 70% or consider using cyclophosphamide at a dose of 1500mg/m²
Ifosfamide: clinical decision, consider using cyclophosphamide at a dose of 1500mg/m²
Liposomal doxorubicin: CrCl < 30: clinical decision

dose_modifications.condition: Palmar-plantar erythrodysaesthesia (PPE) or stomatitis NCI-CTC grade 1 at week 4 or 5
dose_modifications.notes: Grade 1 at week 4: re-dose unless previous grade 3/4 skin toxicity (then wait additional week). Grade 1 at week 5: re-dose unless previous grade 3/4. Grade 1 at week 6: decrease dose by 25% and return to 4-week interval or stop.

dose_modifications.condition: PPE or stomatitis NCI-CTC grade 2 at week 4 or 5
dose_modifications.notes: Grade 2 at week 4: wait additional week. Grade 2 at week 5: wait additional week. Grade 2 at week 6: decrease dose by 25% and return to 4-week interval or stop.

dose_modifications.condition: PPE or stomatitis NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 at week 6
dose_modifications.notes: Grade 3-4 at week 6: stop treatment

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 mucositis/GI toxicity - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 neutropenic sepsis - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence
Reduce to 80% on first occurrence and to 60% on second occurrence
Consider reducing dose for next cycle. Grade 2 or worsening despite dose reduction: consider stopping ifosfamide.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below then reduce to 75% of the original dose or discontinue as appropriate.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: INPATIENT regimen. Urine output must be maintained above 100ml/hour. EDTA or calculated CrCl prior to each cycle. Urine dip for protein every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide. Baseline ECG and LVEF required. Discontinue liposomal doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including uric acid, albumin, calcium, magnesium, bicarbonate and phosphate) prior to day 1
Urine dip test for protein every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours during and day after ifosfamide (urine output > 100ml/hour)
Baseline ECG and LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; discontinue liposomal doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops
special_warnings: INPATIENT REGIMEN - all supportive medicines must be prescribed on inpatient chart
Liposomal doxorubicin is INCOMPATIBLE with sodium chloride 0.9% - use glucose 5% ONLY; always use glucose 5% flush
Do NOT use in-line filters with liposomal doxorubicin
NOT a licensed indication for Caelyx at the time of writing - this protocol may require funding approval
Brands of liposomal doxorubicin may not be interchangeable
Mesna uroprotection required: pre-bolus 600mg/m² + co-infusion 3000mg/m² + post-infusion 1800mg/m² over 12 hours

Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk factors: low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor PS, CNS tumour, bulky pelvic disease, psychotropic drugs, younger age. Methylene blue 50mg four times daily IV in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 30 minutes may prevent or treat.

Liposomal doxorubicin infusion reaction: immediately stop infusion, administer chlorpheniramine and hydrocortisone; restart at 5% total dose over 15 minutes; double rate every 15 minutes if tolerated; total infusion time 90 minutes
Growth factors per local formulary: filgrastim 30MU SC daily from day 6, OR lenograstim 33.6MU SC daily from day 6, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC once on day 4
Palliative intent only

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: EDTA or calculated CrCl required prior to each cycle
required_patient_fields.cardiac_function: Baseline ECG and LVEF required before starting therapy
required_patient_fields.prior_anthracycline_dose_mg_m2: Cumulative prior anthracycline dose (mg/m²) required. Doxorubicin lifetime limit 450-550mg/m² (lower with cardiac risk factors or prior mediastinal radiation). Epirubicin limit 900mg/m².

drugs.special_instructions: Exfoliant. INCOMPATIBLE with sodium chloride 0.9% - use glucose 5% ONLY; always use glucose 5% flush. First infusion: maximum rate 1mg/minute. Subsequent infusions may be given over 60 minutes (default Aria time 120 minutes). Do NOT use in-line filters. Doses >= 90mg: dilute in 500ml glucose 5%. Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Dose banded according to agreed bands. Not a licensed indication for Caelyx.

drugs.special_instructions: Neutral. Mesna uroprotection required. Co-infused with mesna 3000mg/m² in same bag. Dose banded according to agreed bands.
